sanskrit: यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः । न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥
hindiTranslation: जो मनुष्य शास्त्रविधिको छोड़कर अपनी इच्छासे मनमाना आचरण करता है, वह न सिद्धिअन्तःकरणकी शुद्धि को, न सुखको और न परमगतिको ही प्राप्त होता है ॥
englishTranslation: Whoever sets aside scripture and acts on desire alone does not attain perfection, happiness, or the highest state.
essence: Impulse cannot deliver what it promises.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Acting only on impulse, while ignoring wise guidance, blocks real fulfilment, lasting happiness, and the highest good.
simpleInsight: Freedom without guidance is not freedom at all; it can quietly close every real door.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, after warning Arjuna about the three inner gates of ruin, Krishna sharpens the point: a person who throws aside right guidance and follows desire alone will not reach inner fulfilment, happiness, or the highest aim. The battlefield crisis becomes a lesson in disciplined choice.
modernRelevance: You are about to send the text, quit the job, or spend the money because the urge is loud. The verse says impulse can feel decisive while quietly cutting off what you actually want most.
translationLiteral: यः (whoever) / शास्त्रविधिम् (scriptural rule) / उत्सृज्य (having set aside) / वर्तते (acts) / कामकारतः (under the pull of desire) / न (not) / सः (that person) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / अवाप्नोति (attains) / न (not) / सुखम् (happiness) / न (not) / पराम् गतिम् (highest state) ॥
